method_atom: A metric-free, projective-geometric formulation of dynamics on the space of rays (half-lines from the origin) of a finite-dimensional real vector space, in which the state is a ray, the momentum is a bivector whose supporting 2-plane contains the ray, and evolution is generated by a prescribed homogeneous field of bivectors (a 'field of projective forces'). The resulting trajectories are unparameterized curves in projective space, and a divergence-free such field exists only when the ambient space has dimension four.
object_atom: Classical central-force gravitation, specifically the two-body Kepler problem, whose conic-section orbits and hidden (dynamical) symmetries are recovered as the simplest case.
brief_reason: The transferable part is the projective, non-metric, ray-and-bivector dynamical machinery driven by a homogeneous force field; the domain-specific part is the Keplerian gravitational central-force system whose conics and hidden symmetries it reproduces.
